title: carludovica marceana
intro: Carludovica marceana, also known as the Zamia palm or Quique, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, native to Central America, particularly in the tropical regions of Costa Rica and Panama. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The leaves and roots of Carludovica marceana have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing relief from pain and inflammation. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which are often used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
